---
package: "openpipeline_spatial"
version: "v0.5.0"
title: "xenium_spatial_statistics"
namespace: "Feature annotation"
description: "Calculate various spatial statistics from Xenium data including cell morphology ratios, position-based features, local density metrics, and global spatial patterns."
type: "module"
toc: false
latest: "true"
subtitle: "openpipeline_spatial v0.5.0"
---

::: {.column-margin}

### Info

ID: `xenium_spatial_statistics`  
Namespace: `feature_annotation`

### Links

[{{< fa brands github >}} View source code](https://github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline_spatial/tree/v0.5.0/src/feature_annotation/xenium_spatial_statistics)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Browse all versions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline_spatial)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Get run instructions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline_spatial/v0.5.0/components/feature_annotation/xenium_spatial_statistics)

:::

This component expects a MuData object with a pre-computed spatial neighborhood graph
in `.obsp`

## Example commands

You can run the pipeline using `nextflow run`.

### View help

You can use `--help` as a parameter to get an overview of the possible parameters.

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline_spatial \
  -r v0.5.0 -latest \
  -main-script target/nextflow/feature_annotation/xenium_spatial_statistics/main.nf \
  --help
```

### Run command

<details>
<summary>Example of `params.yaml`</summary>

Generate a `params.yaml` for this component on [Viash Hub](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline_spatial/v0.5.0/components/feature_annotation/xenium_spatial_statistics).

</details>

Run locally with:

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline_spatial \
  -r v0.5.0 -latest \
  -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/feature_annotation/xenium_spatial_statistics/main.nf \
  -params-file params.yaml
```

:::{.callout-note}
Replace `-profile docker` with `-profile podman` or `-profile singularity` depending on the desired backend.
:::

Running on Seqera or through Viash Hub is explained in the [guides](/guides/index.qmd).

## Argument groups

### Inputs

|Name                         |Description                                                                                                            |Attributes                                |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--input`                    |A MuData file containing spatial transcriptomics data and a pre-computed spatial neighborhood graph in `.obsp`.        |`file`, required, example: `"input.h5mu"` |
|`--modality`                 |The name of the modality to use from the MuData object. This specifies which AnnData object contains the spatial data. |`string`, required, default: `"rna"`      |
|`--obsm_spatial_coordinates` |The key in `.obsm` where spatial coordinates are stored. Expected shape is (n_cells, 2) for 2D coordinates.            |`string`, default: `"spatial"`            |


### Outputs

|Name              |Description                                                                                                                       |Attributes                       |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--output`        |The output MuData file with calculated spatial statistics. Per-cell metrics are stored in `.obs` and global statistics in `.uns`. |`file`, default: `"output.h5mu"` |
|`--output_prefix` |Prefix to add to all generated column names in `.obs`.                                                                            |`string`, default: `"spatial_"`  |


### Parameters

|Name                   |Description                                                                                                                                       |Attributes                  |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--density_bandwidth`  |Bandwidth parameter for Gaussian kernel density estimation in spatial units (typically microns). Larger values create smoother density estimates. |`double`, default: `50`     |
|`--calculate_ripley_l` |Whether to calculate Ripley's L statistic.  Warning: This is an O(N^2) operation and can be very slow for large datasets (>5000 cells).           |`boolean`, default: `FALSE` |
|`--n_subsample_ripley` |Number of cells to subsample for Ripley's L calculation.  If -1, use all cells. Recommended to keep this below 5000 for performance.              |`integer`, default: `-1`    |
